exemplar.title: Creating a comprehensive guide for families and patients with hemophilia to help management and improvement of quality of life

detailedExemplarData.title: Creating a comprehensive guide for families and patients with hemophilia to help management and improvement of quality of life

strengths: **Interpersonal and communication ATL skills**: You provide clear examples of active listening and adapting your language to elicit deeper patient and practitioner insights, demonstrating effective use of communication skills.

**Information-management and creative-thinking integration**: The OPVL evaluation, brainstorming techniques and persuasive structuring of the guide showcase strong information-management and creative-thinking ATL application.

**Strategic use of technology**: Digitizing the guide with QR-code distribution reflects thoughtful planning for accessibility and shows how information-management skills enhanced the productâs reach and usability.
weaknesses: **Linkage of disciplinary insights to product structure**: You mention pediatric interview knowledge but donât specify how these insights informed particular guide sections or content organization.

**Lack of feedback metrics**: Describing outcomes or feedback statistics (e.g., survey ratings, interview summaries) would strengthen evidence of the ATL skillsâ impact on both the learning process and product quality.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You demonstrate a wide range of ATL skillsâespecially in communication, information management and creative thinkingâwith concrete examples of application. To deepen this section, explicitly tie the disciplinary insights to your guideâs structure and include **quantifiable feedback** to illustrate the real-world impact of these skills.

strengths: **Authentic personal motivation**: The vivid anecdote about your familyâs doctor appointment and the challenges they faced creates a compelling, authentic link between your personal interest and the learning objective.

**Clear connection to context**: You effectively articulate how your familyâs experience motivated the project, grounding your guide in real community needs and strengthening the rationale for the learning goal.
weaknesses: **Lack of measurable success criteria**: The learning objective and product aim are clear but not accompanied by specific, quantifiable benchmarks (e.g., number of sections completed or user satisfaction targets).

**Insufficient action-plan detail**: While tasks and dates are listed, the plan omits concrete deliverables, required resources and logical sequencing (e.g., draft versions, interview transcripts, expert reviews).
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning shows a strong personal rationale and contextual relevance, but it would benefit from transforming general aims into **specific, measurable criteria** and fleshing out your action plan with detailed outputs, resources and timelines to ensure feasibility and track progress effectively.

strengths: **Deep personal reflection**: You thoughtfully explore how empathy and interpersonal understanding grew through the project, highlighting significant personal learning.
**Evidence-based evaluation**: The inclusion of questionnaire results and practical first-aid case examples provides solid evidence of how the product meets user needs and success criteria.
**Self-management insight**: Reflections on improved planning and study techniques demonstrate genuine growth and foresight in applying lessons learned to future work.
weaknesses: **Incomplete criterion-by-criterion assessment**: The reflection summarizes feedback but stops short of explicitly evaluating the product against each predefined success criterion.
**Need for visual enhancement**: You note that the guide lacks illustrations, yet donât propose specific visual elements or infographics that would improve standalone usability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection balances personal growth and product evaluation with concrete evidence from user feedback and case examples. For further depth, perform a **systematic evaluation** against every success criterion and outline targeted visual improvements (e.g., diagrams, flowcharts) to enhance the guideâs clarity and independence.

annotations.comment: You state your learning objective clearly but lack measurable success criteria. Consider defining specific, quantifiable targets (e.g., number of guide sections completed, feedback scores) to track progress.
You effectively link your personal motivation to your project by describing the challenges your family faced, creating a strong foundation for your learning objective.
Your vivid anecdote about the doctorâs appointment brings authenticity to your personal interest and clarifies why this guide is important.
The product objective is clear, but you need detailed, measurable achievement criteria (e.g., number of practical cases included, user satisfaction ratings) to assess success.

Your action plan lists tasks and dates but omits specific deliverables, resources and sequencing details. Add concrete outputs (e.g., draft versions, interview transcripts) and resource requirements per stage.
You provide evidence of active listening, which demonstrates effective interpersonal ATL skills and contributes to gathering detailed qualitative data.
Your reflection on empathy shows deep impact on your personal growth, illustrating how the project enhanced your understanding of diverse perspectives.
Your vivid anecdote about the doctorâs appointment brings authenticity to your personal interest and clarifies why this guide is important.
You effectively link your personal motivation to your project by describing the challenges your family faced, creating a strong foundation for your learning objective.

You state your learning objective clearly but lack measurable success criteria. Consider defining specific, quantifiable targets (e.g., number of guide sections completed, feedback scores) to track progress.
The product objective is clear, but you need detailed, measurable achievement criteria (e.g., number of practical cases included, user satisfaction ratings) to assess success.

Your action plan lists tasks and dates but omits specific deliverables, resources and sequencing details. Add concrete outputs (e.g., draft versions, interview transcripts) and resource requirements per stage.
Strong use of communication ATL: you describe how adapting your language and question style elicited relevant practitioner and patient insights, directly supporting your learning objective.
Your OPVL table reflects a thorough information-management ATL: you critically evaluated sources for origin, value and limitations, strengthening your guideâs credibility.
Clear explanation of your personal interest and community need demonstrates a convincing connection between your learning goal and your personal context.
Good transfer of ATL skills from Language and Literature: you applied text-fluency and persuasive communication techniques to organize and structure your guide for maximum impact.
Excellent application of information-management ATL to product: you digitized the guide and used social media distribution to enhance accessibility and reach.
Your use of brainstorming to clarify and structure ideas illustrates a strong creative-thinking ATL, crucial for defining your project topic and objectives.
Your evaluation section summarises user feedback but doesnât explicitly rate each success criterion. Include a detailed assessment against all criteria for a thorough reflection.
You mention transferring disciplinary knowledge from pediatric interviews but could clarify how these insights shaped specific guide sections or structure.
Linking creative thinking and information management shows how ideation and execution combined to produce your innovative guide design.
Considering environmental impact by offering QR-code digital guides demonstrates strategic ATL use and adds value to the product.
You effectively reflect on improved self-management and study techniques, demonstrating how the project shaped your future planning and responsibility.
You include detailed practical first-aid cases, which help users apply the guide effectively; these examples strengthen the guideâs real-world utility.
The product description is comprehensive but lacks distinct achievement criteria. Define clear, measurable benchmarks (e.g., number of expert reviews completed).
The questionnaire results provide concrete evidence that your guide meets practical applicability criteria, showing you evaluated the product against success criteria.
Your reflection identifies a lack of illustrations affecting guide autonomy. Consider adding clear visual aids and infographics to ensure the guide stands alone without your explanation.
While you describe adapting language to each audience, adding feedback metrics or interview outcomes would strengthen evidence of ATL effectiveness.
